# UC-22.23.2 · Default Credential and Hardcoded Secret Detection in Releases

## Description

Supports CRA product security, vulnerability handling, and transparency obligations with auditable telemetry from engineering and incident processes.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU CRA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes product_security and sourcetypes cicd:build:log, github:actions. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review. Indexes required: `index=product_security`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cicd:build:log`, `sourcetype=github:actions`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for GitHub (5765).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=product_security` `cicd:build:log` — product_id, release_id, cve_id, status, result, enisa_ticket_id.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=product_security (sourcetype="cicd:build:log" OR sourcetype="github:actions") earliest=-30d
| eval cra_tag="secrets"
| eval uc_id="22.23.2"
| stats count as events, sum(eval(status="fail" OR result="fail")) as fails, dc(release_id) as releases by product_id, cra_tag
| where fails>0
| sort - fails
| head 200
```

Understanding this SPL

**Default Credential and Hardcoded Secret Detection in Releases (EU CRA)** — Supports CRA product security, vulnerability handling, and transparency obligations with auditable telemetry from engineering and incident processes.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=product_security` `cicd:build:log` — product_id, release_id, cve_id, status, result, enisa_ticket_id. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for GitHub (5765). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: product_security; **sourcetype**: cicd:build:log, github:actions. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=product_security, sourcetype="cicd:build:log", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **cra_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_id** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by product_id, cra_tag** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where fails>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Limits the number of rows with `head`.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart (fails), Table (product_id), Single value (releases with fails)

## SPL

```spl
index=product_security (sourcetype="cicd:build:log" OR sourcetype="github:actions") earliest=-30d
| eval cra_tag="secrets"
| eval uc_id="22.23.2"
| stats count as events, sum(eval(status="fail" OR result="fail")) as fails, dc(release_id) as releases by product_id, cra_tag
| where fails>0
| sort - fails
| head 200
```

## Visualization

Time chart (fails), Table (product_id), Single value (releases with fails)
